layouts.bitOrder: MSB-first within each little-endian uint32 word
layouts.scope: Verified by matching the serialized 128-byte flag pages in USER_DATA000 record 1000 to the corresponding read-only event-flag pages in a running Sekiro 1.06 process. The mapped Gourd Seed and Prayer Bead flags reconcile exactly with inventory-derived totals in the reference, After Divine Dragon, and Before Isshin fixtures.

evidence.details: The process was opened with PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION | PROCESS_VM_READ only. No writes, injection, suspension, or game/save modifications were performed. Non-zero 0x80-byte runtime flag pages matched USER_DATA000 at record-relative page offsets beginning at 0x34.
SoulSplitter documents the EventFlagMan bank selection, FieldArea world-block category lookup, last-three-digit word selection, and MSB-first uint32 bit mask used by Sekiro 1.06.
Primary location flags produce 7/9 and 26/40 for the reference fixture, 8/9 and 28/40 after Divine Dragon, and 8/9 and 33/40 before Isshin, exactly matching the independent inventory-derived counts.

limitations: The bank and world-category table covers Sekiro 1.06's serialized flag layout. Event IDs in unallocated banks or unmapped world blocks return unknown instead of reading unrelated bytes.
